quick_facts.label: Core function
quick_facts.value: Plans and performs scheduled, preventive and breakdown maintenance to keep industrial machinery and production equipment available and functioning.

quick_facts.label: Common entry preparation
quick_facts.value: Relevant pathways include an engineering diploma or degree followed by practical training, or ITI/CTS trade training such as Fitter or Mechanic Machine Tool Maintenance; the applicable route depends on the employer, equipment and sector.

quick_facts.label: Work pattern
quick_facts.value: The role is normally hands-on rather than desk-based and may involve shift work, overtime, travel and coordination with helpers or technicians, depending on the plant and employer.

quick_facts.label: Indicative progression
quick_facts.value: A related National Career Service maintenance pathway places Maintenance Executive between Worker/Helper and Senior Maintenance Executive, with Maintenance Manager above; NCS labels this progression indicative.

sections.heading: What a Maintenance Engineer does
paragraphs: A Maintenance Engineer organizes and executes planned maintenance, responds to faults and breakdowns, and works to restore equipment so production can continue reliably. The closely related NCS Maintenance Executive profile explicitly includes scheduled maintenance, routine, preventive and breakdown maintenance, and ensuring trouble-free production.

Maintenance work also includes practical inspection, repair and troubleshooting. The Directorate General of Training’s Fitter curriculum covers day-to-day preventive maintenance, checking machine functionality, repair work, bearings, lubrication, and troubleshooting pneumatic and hydraulic-system faults.
bullets: Create and follow preventive-maintenance schedules and job plans.
Investigate equipment faults, isolate the cause and coordinate safe repair or replacement.
Inspect mechanical, electrical, pneumatic or hydraulic components according to the plant’s equipment scope.
Record work, technical observations and maintenance activity in job cards, logs or other plant documentation.
Support production continuity while following site safety procedures and standard operating practices.

sections.heading: Entry routes in India
paragraphs: There is no single nationally prescribed qualification for the broad title “Maintenance Engineer”; employers match qualifications to the equipment and sector. A common graduate route is an engineering degree or diploma followed by supervised, work-based learning. The Ministry of Education’s National Apprenticeship Training Scheme is available to fresh engineering degree and diploma pass-outs and is designed to provide paid on-the-job training in factories or workplaces.

A technician-to-engineer route can begin with ITI/CTS preparation in a relevant trade. DGT lists Mechanic Machine Tool Maintenance as a two-year, NSQF Level 4 CTS trade for candidates who have passed Class 10 with Science and Mathematics or an equivalent vocational subject. The related NCS maintenance profile identifies ITI training as preferable for entry-level maintenance work.
bullets: Diploma/degree route: pursue a relevant engineering discipline, then seek maintenance, plant-engineering or graduate/technician apprenticeship opportunities.
ITI/CTS route: train in a machinery, fitting, electrical, instrumentation, refrigeration or maintenance-related trade appropriate to the target industry.
Build supervised hands-on experience through apprenticeship, industrial training, or an entry role such as technician, helper or junior maintenance staff.
Check each vacancy’s required discipline, experience, licence or safety training; requirements differ across manufacturing, utilities, buildings, railways and process industries.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The technical mix depends on the plant, but maintenance practice requires systematic inspection, fault diagnosis, safe repair and documentation. DGT’s Fitter curriculum includes preventive maintenance, repair, bearings, lubrication, pneumatic and hydraulic troubleshooting, and technical forms, process charts, activity logs and job cards.

National Occupational Standards are a useful benchmark for competency-oriented training: NSDC describes them as standards of workplace performance, knowledge and understanding, while Qualification Packs group standards for a particular job role.
bullets: Mechanical maintenance: fittings, bearings, joints, lubrication, alignment and basic machine repair.
Fault-finding: identify failures caused by leakage, contamination, friction, improper mounting, cavitation or component wear.
Fluid-power and controls awareness: pneumatic and hydraulic-system maintenance and troubleshooting; develop electrical or instrumentation capability where the plant requires it.
Safe working discipline: apply plant procedures, isolation practices, PPE requirements and applicable occupational-safety rules.
Communication and records: write clear job-card and activity-log entries; coordinate with production personnel, technicians, contractors and supervisors.

sections.heading: Where and how the work is performed
paragraphs: Maintenance Engineers work around operating equipment rather than exclusively at a desk. In the related NCS profile, probable settings include electronic-hardware companies, manufacturing facilities and maintenance companies; it also notes possible shifts, overtime, travel and team supervision. The exact environment varies substantially by the asset base—for example, machine tools, plant utilities, process equipment, material-handling systems or building services.

Safety is integral because maintenance involves industrial worksites and equipment. India’s Ministry of Labour and Employment lists the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020 among the national labour-code materials; site-specific rules, permits and procedures remain important in day-to-day work.
bullets: Manufacturing plants and assembly facilities.
Industrial utilities, maintenance contractors and equipment-service organizations.
Potential shift-based or breakdown-response coverage where plants operate continuously.
Cross-functional coordination with production, stores/spares, quality, contractors and maintenance technicians.

sections.heading: Progression opportunities
paragraphs: Progression generally comes through greater equipment responsibility, stronger fault-analysis capability, planning responsibility and leadership of technicians or contractors. NCS presents an indicative related pathway from Worker/Helper to Maintenance Executive, Senior Maintenance Executive and Maintenance Manager. Titles are not standardized, so a Maintenance Engineer may move toward senior maintenance, reliability, planning, utilities, facility engineering or maintenance-management work depending on the industry.

For degree and diploma holders, NATS can provide a structured early-career bridge: the Board of Apprenticeship Training describes it as paid on-the-job training in government or private organizations and states that successful completion leads to a Government of India Certificate of Proficiency considered equivalent to one year of experience.
bullets: Entry support roles: helper, trainee, technician or apprentice.
Independent maintenance roles: maintenance engineer or senior maintenance engineer.
Coordination and planning roles: maintenance planner, reliability-focused engineer, shift or area lead.
Leadership roles: maintenance supervisor or maintenance manager.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Pay is highly variable by location, sector, shift pattern, qualification, equipment specialization and experience. As a dated reference only, the NCS Maintenance Executive profile—last updated on December 26, 2018—listed indicative new-entrant earnings of ₹15,000–₹19,000 per month and expressly stated that the figures were subject to change. It should not be treated as a current Maintenance Engineer salary benchmark.

For apprentices, remuneration is a stipend rather than a regular salary. The Board of Apprenticeship Training states that NATS is paid on-the-job training and that the Government prescribes a minimum stipend rate while establishments may pay above it; candidates should confirm the current applicable stipend and employment terms directly in the specific apprenticeship or job notice.
bullets: Treat any advertised package as vacancy-specific rather than as a nationwide rate.
Compare fixed pay, shift allowance, overtime terms, insurance and statutory benefits where applicable.
Confirm whether an offer is an apprenticeship stipend, fixed-term engagement or regular employment before accepting.
